address: Chang Khlan Road, Muang Chiang Mai

description.en:
## Overview

Chiang Mai Night Bazaar is a sprawling open-air market that stretches along Chang Khlan Road in the heart of the city. Open every night of the year, it is one of the most popular evening activities in Chiang Mai and draws a steady stream of tourists and locals alike. The market centers on the intersection of Chang Khlan Road and Loi Khro Road and extends roughly one kilometer in each direction, filling the sidewalks and side streets with hundreds of stalls.

## History

The Night Bazaar traces its origins to the ancient Yunnanese trading caravans that once traveled through Chiang Mai along overland trade routes connecting China to the Andaman Sea. The modern market grew organically during the 1980s and 1990s as tourism boomed in northern Thailand. Today it occupies a permanent stretch of real estate that includes open-air stalls, covered market halls like the Kalare Night Bazaar and Anusarn Market, and the surrounding restaurants and bars that line the road.

## What to See

The bazaar is a paradise for shoppers looking for handicrafts, clothing, souvenirs, and local art. Chiang Mai artisans are known for hand-carved wooden items, traditional Thai umbrellas from Bo Sang, silver jewelry, handwoven silk scarves, and colorful hill-tribe textiles. Street food vendors serve up classic northern Thai dishes including Khao Soi, Sai Ua sausage, grilled meat skewers, Pad Thai, mango sticky rice, and fresh tropical fruit smoothies. The covered Kalare Night Bazaar building often hosts live music and traditional Thai dance performances in its open-air courtyard.

## Practical Info

The market is open nightly from roughly 6:00 PM to midnight and is free to enter. Most vendors accept only cash, so bring plenty of Thai baht. Bargaining is expected at most stalls, especially for clothing and souvenirs. Start at about half the asking price and aim to settle around 60 to 70 percent. Weekday evenings are noticeably less crowded than weekends. The Night Bazaar is within walking distance of many central hotels and is easily reached by songthaew or tuk-tuk for 20 to 40 THB from the Old City.
description.nl: Bruisende nachtmarkt met souvenirs kleding en lokaal straatvoedsel

best_time_to_visit: Weekday evenings around 7-8 PM when stalls are fully set up but crowds are manageable
tips: Bring cash in Thai baht as most vendors do not accept cards
Bargain for handicrafts and clothing but food prices are usually fixed
Visit the Kalare Night Bazaar courtyard for live music and Thai dance performances
